era_context: Ashurnasirpal II is in the early-to-middle years of his reign (883–859 BCE), actively consolidating Assyrian power through military campaigns and monumental construction. The Near East is experiencing a period of imperial expansion. Greece is in the Archaic period, emerging from the Dark Ages. Egypt is fragmented under the Third Intermediate Period. China's Western Zhou is approaching its final century.

events.title: Ashurnasirpal II's military campaigns in the Levant
events.description: Ashurnasirpal II continues his systematic military campaigns to expand Assyrian control over the Levantine city-states. These campaigns are designed to extract tribute, establish vassal relationships, and suppress any resistance to Assyrian authority. The campaigns are characterized by the use of overwhelming military force and the strategic destruction of rebellious cities. By 882 BCE, Ashurnasirpal II has established Assyrian dominance over much of northern Syria and Mesopotamia.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Assyrian and Levantine sites

events.certainty_note: Ashurnasirpal II's campaigns are well-documented in contemporary inscriptions.

events.title: Nimrud palace complex under construction
events.description: The construction of the Northwest Palace at Nimrud continues under Ashurnasirpal II's direction. The palace is being decorated with elaborate stone reliefs and inscriptions that document the king's military achievements and divine favor. The palace serves as both a royal residence and a center of administrative power, symbolizing Assyrian imperial authority.
events.certainty_note: The palace complex is archaeologically well-attested.

events.title: Western Zhou dynasty in decline
events.description: The Western Zhou dynasty continues its gradual decline as regional lords accumulate power and the central authority of the Zhou king weakens. By 882 BCE, the feudal system is well-established, with vassal states operating with increasing independence. The process of fragmentation that will lead to the Spring and Autumn period is well underway.
events.certainty_note: The general trajectory is well-established, but specific events in 882 BCE are not precisely documented.

disconfirming_evidence: No significant disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 882 BCE follows the same pattern as the previous two years: rich for Assyria, sparse for other regions. The historical record is heavily weighted toward the Near East.

graph_edges.note: Ashurnasirpal II's campaigns continue across multiple years
